title: Difference between podman machine set --rootful and podman system connection default podman-machine-default-root?
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I just upgraded to podman 4.0.2 (on macOS). When I start the machine, I see the following:

> This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
> require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
> issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command: 
> 
> 	podman machine set --rootful

In the past, I had run the following command to switch to rootful:

```
podman system connection default podman-machine-default-root
```

So I ran `podman system connection default podman-machine-default-root`, and then I see that I am `root` as expected:

```
$ podman run --rm fedora whoami
root
```

However, if I stop the machine and restart it, I still see the message about `podman machine set --rootful`.

What's the difference between `podman machine set --rootful` and `podman system connection default podman-machine-default-root`?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine init`
2. `podman machine start`

**Describe the results you received:**

Confusing help message on `podman machine start`

**Describe the results you expected:**

Unclear

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

comments.body:
I guess there are identical, no idea why an extra command for this was added.
cc @n1hility

It adds an authoritative source for the API forwarding service (/var/run/docker.sock, $HOME/[...]/[machine name].sock), which needs to know which to point to. The connection info doesn't necessarily determine this (you can have only one default but N machines each with different rootful/rootless settings, you can have a default pointing to an external system etc). However, the goal is to synchronize the two where possible. So when you do a --set it will move the connection for you to point to root/rootful  if it is pointing to the current machine. If not it just moves the API service for that machine instance. This info will also be useful for a future podman machine env command, which needs to know which CONTAINER_HOST and DOCKER_HOST settings are required.

I see, I guess this should be added in https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-machine-set.1.md.

@kgibm Also `podman run --rm fedora whoami` will always show you root since this is inside the container. The difference about root/rootless is if podman itself is run as root or rootless user in the VM.

@kgibm yes this is new in 4 so if you are building automation for 3 and 4, what I would recommend doing is to prefer setting it via init or set and just having a fallback piece of logic if you get a non-zero exit code. Although if you aren't using API forwarding then you can still do it the old way with 4. Over time other things may reference the stored config setting.

@Luap99 there is a description there about how it syncs connections in the --rootful option. Where you thinking we should add some sort of explicit note to recommend using this over podman system connection?

> @Luap99 there is a description there about how it syncs connections in the --rootful option. Where you thinking we should add some sort of explicit note to recommend using this over podman system connection?

Yes something like that but also `API forwarding, if available, will follow this setting` did not make me think of the different sockets.
